reason: Provenance gap on '||Phi||=1 in the order-unit norm'. The node attributes this contractivity fact to GT-positive-unital (refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md), but Idel 2013 does NOT contain it. Idel Definition 2.1 (idel-2013.md:345-349) supplies only positivity (A>=0 => T(A)>=0), unitality (T(1)=1), and Hermitian-preservation; the document has ZERO occurrences of 'order unit'/'order norm' (grep -c = 0) and never states a unital positive map is order-unit-norm contractive or that ||Phi||=1 in any norm. The only ||Phi||=1 in ground truth is Kitaev (approximate_algebras.tex:359), the COMPLETELY-BOUNDED norm ||Phi||_cb=1, which GT-kitaev-spectral explicitly flags as 'Kitaev works in cb-norm; our adaptation re-derives the bounds in the operator/order-unit norm.' Node 1.1 neither cites a valid source for order-unit-norm contractivity nor re-derives it. The standard derivation (||a||<=1 => -I<=a<=I; apply positive unital Phi to get -I<=Phi(a)<=I, hence ||Phi(a)||<=1; with Phi(I)=I gives =1) needs GT-order-unit-norm (HOS joa-m.md:368-372), which the node does not invoke. Since ||S||<=2||Phi||+||I||=3 rests entirely on ||Phi||=1, this unprovenanced fact taints the spectral bound.
